task_completion.description: The 'Employee Info' field group is created using the Advanced Custom Fields plugin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the WordPress admin dashboard with the Advanced Custom Fields (ACF) interface, specifically editing or creating a field group titled 'Employee Info'.

task_completion.description: The three required text fields ('Job Title', 'Department', 'Office Extension') are added to the field group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the ACF field group editor showing the fields 'Job Title', 'Department', and 'Office Extension' added to the 'Employee Info' group.

task_completion.description: The field group is configured to display for posts in the 'Team' category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the Location Rules section in the ACF field group editor showing a rule configured as 'Post Category' 'is equal to' 'Team'.

task_completion.description: A post for 'Emily Chen' is published in the 'Team' category with the correct custom field values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the WordPress post editor for 'Emily Chen' showing the 'Team' category selected, and the custom fields filled with 'Technical Director' (Job Title), 'Engineering' (Department), and 'x402' (Office Extension).

task_completion.description: A post for 'Marcus Johnson' is published in the 'Team' category with the correct custom field values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the WordPress post editor for 'Marcus Johnson' showing the 'Team' category selected, and the custom fields filled with 'Creative Lead' (Job Title), 'Design' (Department), and 'x415' (Office Extension).

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress admin GUI to create the field group and posts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the WordPress admin dashboard in a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands (like WP-CLI) to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing `wp post create` or `wp db query` commands for the main tasks.

integrity.description: The agent actually typed the information into the fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the input fields, typing titles, and filling out the custom fields in the browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected plugin: 'advanced-custom-fields'. Target category: 'Team'. Expected employee posts: 1) 'Emily Chen' (Job Title: Technical Director, Department: Engineering, Office Extension: x402). 2) 'Marcus Johnson' (Job Title: Creative Lead, Department: Design, Office Extension: x415).
